opening_complaint.verbatim_patient_text: I can't catch my breath when I lie down, my chest feels like it's clamped in a vice, and I've been hacking a dry cough non-stop for about twelve hours. I had radiation treatment for lung cancer a couple of months back, and my blue puffer isn't doing a thing.
opening_complaint.stated_reason_for_presenting_today: It's got a lot worse tonight — I can't lie down or sleep, the coughing won't stop, and I feel like I'm not getting enough air.

symptom_narrative.onset: the breathlessness crept in about two weeks ago, only when I moved around at first; it got a lot worse over the last twelve hours
symptom_narrative.duration: roughly two weeks, with a sharp worsening in the last half-day
symptom_narrative.character: a dry, harsh, hacking cough that brings nothing up, and a tight, locked feeling in my chest
symptom_narrative.severity_patient_description: the worst it's been — I can only sit up in the chair, I can't lie down at all and I can barely finish a sentence without coughing
symptom_narrative.timing_pattern: there all the time now and getting worse; worse when I lie flat
symptom_narrative.what_makes_it_better: sitting bolt upright helps a little; sipping water
symptom_narrative.what_makes_it_worse: lying down, and any moving around
symptom_narrative.associated_symptoms_volunteered: I've felt feverish and run-down for a couple of weeks, but I'm not bringing up any phlegm and there's no blood
symptom_narrative.previous_episodes: nothing like this before the radiation
symptom_narrative.functional_impact: I've been stuck in the chair all night, exhausted, can't sleep for the coughing

past_medical_history.condition_as_patient_states: lung cancer, treated with a course of radiation to the chest
past_medical_history.when_diagnosed: diagnosed earlier this year; finished the radiation about ten weeks ago
past_medical_history.how_managed: under the cancer specialists; radiation now completed

social_history_volunteered.relevant_life_context: lives alone, no one else in the house tonight, exhausted from continuous coughing and no sleep

communication_style.typical_message_style: short phrases broken up by coughing

objective_data_offered.value: oxygen reading 92% on a home fingertip oximeter
objective_data_offered.reliability_caveat: consumer fingertip device, not calibrated; single reading

objective_data_offered.value: pulse showing about 105 on the same fingertip oximeter
visibly working hard to breathe, shoulders lifting with each breath, speaking only in short phrases broken by a dry cough
cannot lie flat at all — sitting bolt upright in a chair to breathe
a reddish-brown discoloured, slightly irritated patch of skin on the right front of the chest over the area where the radiation was given
